articles.body: Getting Started with SoulShine AI: Creating an Account and Integrating Grace or Levi SoulShine is built to help ministries move faster without losing theological clarity or pastoral warmth. Most teams begin by choosing the one assistant or workflow that will remove the most pressure first, then expanding after the first use case is stable. Choose Your Starting Point Grace is usually the best first step if your team needs a 24/7 digital front door, message triage, prayer support, or congregation-facing communication help. Levi is usually the best first step if your team is overloaded by sermon-aligned content, discipleship materials, planning, or communications prep. If you are not sure where to begin, start with the workflow that is currently consuming the most staff time every week. Recommended First-Week Setup Create the ministry account using the email your team will actually manage long term. Complete the ministry profile with your church or ministry name, primary contact details, and basic organizational context. Upload a small set of foundational documents before expecting polished responses. Decide who should review outputs, tune tone, and handle human follow-ups. Connect one channel or content workflow first instead of trying to launch everything at once. What to Prepare Before You Go Live Your statement of faith or doctrinal guidance Current sermon transcripts, teaching notes, or ministry FAQs Default handoff language for pastoral or sensitive conversations Clear ownership for billing, setup, and support questions Best Practice A smaller, clean rollout is better than a broad rollout built on outdated or conflicting source material. Start narrow, review results, then expand.
articles.title: Getting Started with SoulShine AI: Creating an Account and Integrating Grace or Levi

articles.body: Welcome to SoulShine AI SoulShine is a faith-aligned AI platform built for ministries that need operational help without sacrificing trust, theological clarity, or human care. The goal is not to replace ministry leaders. The goal is to reduce repetitive workload, increase responsiveness, and support healthier handoffs between digital systems and real people. How SoulShine Is Commonly Used Grace supports congregation-facing communication, spiritual support, and first-line message handling. Levi supports internal content creation, ministry planning, and communication prep. Foundational documents and guardrails help both assistants reflect your ministry's voice rather than sounding generic. What Success Looks Like Routine questions are handled faster Staff regain time for pastoral care and leadership work AI-generated responses stay closer to your ministry's tone and teaching Sensitive conversations are escalated to humans instead of being mishandled How to Get the Most Value Early Choose one high-pressure use case first. Load current ministry documents before broad launch. Test for tone, accuracy, and escalation behavior. Refine based on real examples from your team. Need Help? Use the support portal or email support@soulshineai.com if you need help with setup, billing, routing, or assistant behavior.
articles.title: Welcome to SoulShine AI

articles.body: Setting Up Your Ministry Profile Your ministry profile gives SoulShine the basic context it needs to communicate clearly and serve the right people in the right way. A strong profile improves relevance, while a thin or outdated profile makes the assistants sound generic or miss important context. Information Worth Including Ministry or church name and public-facing contact details Location, service cadence, and basic visitor information Faith tradition, denominational context, or theological lane Core ministries, recurring programs, and common congregation questions Preferred tone for public communication What This Profile Should Not Try to Do It should not replace foundational documents. It should not contain private counseling notes or unnecessary sensitive data. It should not become a dumping ground for every historical document your team has ever produced. Recommended Setup Sequence Enter the current public-facing ministry basics. Describe the audiences you serve and the language you usually use. Add the most common questions people ask your church or ministry. Review the profile whenever schedules, ministries, or leadership messaging changes. Support Tip If Grace or Levi keeps missing basic ministry context, review the ministry profile first, then compare it with your uploaded foundational documents to see what is missing or out of date.
articles.title: Setting Up Your Ministry Profile

articles.body: Getting Started with Soul 24/7 This guide covers the first-time setup flow in Soul 24/7, from the invitation link through the Daily Briefing. If you need help at any point, use the SoulShine support portal or email support@soulshineai.com. Before You Start Have your Soul 24/7 invitation email ready. Use the email address the invitation was sent to. Use the temporary password or sign-in details that came with the invitation. If the invitation link opens a sign-in page with your information already filled in, that is expected. Step 1: Sign In from the Invitation Open the invitation link and sign in. Your email and temporary password may already be filled in, and you can select Sign In to continue. Step 2: Complete My Personal Details Most new accounts start on Getting Started: My Personal Details. This page includes first name, last name, user name, a location toggle, optional profile details, and notification preferences. Note: If some profile details are already saved on your account, Soul 24/7 may start you on My Persona instead. Step 3: Complete My Persona The next step is Getting Started: My Persona. This page asks for background that helps shape the experience, including denomination, Bible version, values, and personal context. You can move forward even if you are not ready to answer every field right away. Step 4: Review My Inspirational Goals After My Persona, Soul 24/7 opens Getting Started: My Inspirational Goals. Here you can review suggested goals, choose a recommendation, or use Add Goal to create one manually. Add Goal opens a simple form with one prompt: What is the goal? Step 5: Review My Growth Plans The next step is My Growth Plans. Here you can review the empty-state plan area, use Add Plan to start a new plan, or continue without creating a plan on the first visit. Add Plan opens a simple prompt asking what you want to accomplish with the plan. Step 6: Finish and Open the Daily Briefing When you finish the guided onboarding flow, Soul 24/7 opens the Daily Briefing. This is the main working screen for the app. If you leave onboarding and later need to get back to the main workspace, tap Briefing in the navigation. Where to Find My Account Later After setup, you can open the side menu and go to My Account. From there you can review your profile, open AI personalization, review goals and growth plans, and restart onboarding. Common Questions Do I have to finish every field the first time through? No. The current setup flow allows you to move forward even if you leave some sections unfinished. What if the app starts on My Persona instead of My Personal Details? That usually means some basic account details are already on file. You can keep going from there. Do I have to create a goal or a plan before finishing? No. Both Add Goal and Add Plan are available, but neither one blocked forward progress during testing. What screen should I use as my main home screen? Use Daily Briefing. That is the main working view in Soul 24/7. When to Contact Support Use the support portal or email support if the invitation link is expired, the login does not work, the onboarding flow will not advance, or you need help getting back to the Daily Briefing. https://soulshineai.freshdesk.com/support/home support@soulshineai.com
articles.title: Getting Started with Soul 24/7

articles.body: SoulShine AI Platform Overview SoulShine is best understood as a ministry operating layer rather than a single chatbot. The platform combines assistant workflows, foundational-document grounding, safety boundaries, and support processes so your team can move faster without losing pastoral judgment. Main Product Lanes Grace supports front-door ministry communication, prayer support, and congregation-facing interactions. Levi supports sermon-aligned content, ministry planning, and internal communications work. Broader SoulShine offerings extend into discipleship, ongoing engagement, and more complete digital ministry workflows depending on what your team adopts. What Makes the Platform Work Well Current foundational documents Clear theological and pastoral guardrails A defined handoff path for sensitive issues One or two focused use cases before expanding further How Teams Commonly Roll Out Choose the assistant or workflow that solves the biggest current bottleneck. Load ministry context and review sample outputs. Test a real but limited use case. Expand to more channels, content types, or ministry functions after the first workflow is stable. Important Boundary SoulShine can accelerate ministry operations, but it should not be treated as a substitute for pastoral authority, crisis response, or sensitive counseling decisions.
articles.title: SoulShine AI Platform Overview

articles.body: Dashboard Navigation Guide The SoulShine workspace may evolve over time, but most teams will see a consistent pattern: account-level setup, assistant configuration, content or conversation workflows, and support or review steps. This guide is meant to orient your team without locking you to labels that may change as the platform matures. Areas Your Team Will Usually Work In Account and profile settings for core ministry details, users, and contact information Assistant configuration for Grace, Levi, channel setup, tone, and guardrails Content or conversation workspaces where your team reviews outputs and manages activity Support and troubleshooting flows when something needs billing, technical, or behavior review How to Orient a New Team Member Show them where ministry profile and foundational documents live. Show them which workflows are customer-facing versus internal-only. Clarify who owns approvals, handoffs, and post-launch adjustments. Keep one documented internal process for support requests so changes do not get lost. Best Practice If a navigation label changes, stay focused on the underlying workflow: profile, grounding documents, guardrails, live usage, and support. That framing is more durable than memorizing menu names. Downloadable Resources Platform Dashboard and Briefing Guide (PDF guide) Platform Dashboard and Briefing Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Dashboard Navigation Guide

articles.body: Uploading Foundational Documents and Training Your Ministry Voice SoulShine works best when Grace and Levi are grounded in the real language, theology, and ministry practices of your church or organization. Foundational documents help the assistants answer in a way that sounds like your ministry instead of sounding generic. What to Upload First Statement of faith or doctrinal summary so the assistant stays aligned on core beliefs Sermon transcripts, teaching notes, or discipleship guides so the writing and examples reflect your voice Ministry FAQs for service times, events, care processes, volunteer pathways, and visitor questions Policies and pastoral boundaries so sensitive topics are handled the way your team expects Referral resources such as counseling contacts, emergency guidance, or local support information Before You Upload Remove duplicate, outdated, or conflicting documents Use clear titles and dates so your team knows what is current Avoid uploading anything you would not want referenced in a live interaction Keep highly sensitive personal data out of training documents unless your team has explicitly approved it Recommended Rollout Sequence Start with your statement of faith and ministry overview Add 3 to 5 representative sermons or teaching resources Add your most common admin and visitor questions Review a sample of Grace and Levi outputs before expanding further Refine your documents when you notice missing context, tone drift, or repeated misunderstandings Best Practices Use current documents, not archives you no longer teach from Prefer a few strong source documents over a large dump of unreviewed material Keep your doctrinal guidance direct and plain-language Review foundational documents whenever leadership, ministry language, or program structure changes When to Ask Support for Help Open a support ticket if the assistant is missing your intended tone, citing outdated material, or handling doctrinal questions in a way that does not match your ministry. Include which assistant is affected, a short example, and which foundational document should have guided the response. Downloadable Resources Foundational Documents Playbook (PDF guide) Foundational Documents Playbook Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Uploading Foundational Documents and Training Your Ministry Voice

articles.body: Security, Privacy, and Data Handling Overview SoulShine is positioned as a trust-first platform for ministries. The public Security Center emphasizes that ministry data is treated as confidential, protected with strong security controls, and not treated as a product to be sold or monetized. Core Security Commitments Highlighted by SoulShine Data confidentiality - your ministry's information is treated as your ministry's data Encryption - the site states data is protected with AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit Role-based access - access should be limited to the right people for the right purposes Plain-language trust posture - pricing, terms, and security expectations should be understandable by ministry leaders and boards Good Internal Practices for Customers Only upload documents that are needed for ministry operations Keep private counseling notes, highly sensitive pastoral records, and unnecessary personal data out of shared training material Limit account access to staff who actively manage SoulShine Review connected channels and integrations during onboarding and after staffing changes Use separate internal processes for emergencies, mandated reporting, and other high-risk situations Questions Ministry Leaders Often Ask Can we tailor the platform to our theology? Yes. Foundational documents and guardrail settings are meant to anchor the assistant to your ministry's teaching and tone. Should we put everything into the system? No. Start with the minimum set of current documents needed for safe, useful answers. What belongs in a support ticket? Configuration questions, safety concerns, access issues, and specific examples of outputs that need review. When to Open a Security / Compliance Ticket Use the Security / Compliance ticket type if your church, nonprofit, or board needs clarification on privacy posture, access expectations, ministry data handling, or a concerning interaction that may involve sensitive information.
articles.title: Security, Privacy, and Data Handling Overview

articles.body: How Theological Safety, Guardrails, and Human Handoffs Work SoulShine's public site places heavy emphasis on faith alignment. In practice, that means the assistant should be guided by your ministry's documents, respect the boundaries you set, and know when to step aside for a human pastor or staff member. Three Layers of Safety Foundational documents shape the assistant's voice, doctrine, and ministry context Guardrails define what the assistant should answer, how deeply it should go, and which topics require caution Human handoffs route sensitive or high-risk conversations to a person instead of forcing the AI to keep going Examples of Topics That Usually Need Tighter Guardrails Denominational distinctives and doctrinal edge cases Crisis language, self-harm, abuse, or immediate danger Marriage conflict, mental health, grief, and trauma-heavy care conversations Questions where your ministry wants the AI to defer to a pastor by default How Human Handoffs Should Work Grace or Levi identifies a topic outside the allowed boundary The assistant responds with a warm, ministry-appropriate handoff message The conversation is routed to the right staff member or support process Your team follows up with the person directly when needed Recommended Setup Approach Define what the assistant can answer directly Define what it can answer lightly and then hand off Define what it should never handle without a person involved Review a sample set of live or test conversations before broad rollout Support Tip If Grace or Levi is going too far, not handing off soon enough, or refusing too much, send support the exact prompt or conversation snippet and the response you wanted instead. That gives the fastest path to better guardrail tuning.
articles.title: How Theological Safety, Guardrails, and Human Handoffs Work

articles.body: Meet Grace - Your Spiritual Support Assistant Grace is SoulShine's congregation-facing assistant. She is designed to help ministries stay responsive, compassionate, and consistent across routine support, prayer-oriented interactions, and front-door communication. Where Grace Usually Helps Most Answering common questions from visitors or members Providing warm first-response support outside staff hours Routing prayer requests and care conversations toward the right next step Reducing repetitive inbox or website-message workload for staff What Grace Needs in Order to Work Well Clear foundational documents Defined doctrinal and pastoral boundaries Compassionate handoff language for sensitive cases A real person or process available when Grace should escalate What Grace Should Not Be Asked to Do Alone Handle emergencies or immediate-danger situations Replace a pastor, counselor, or crisis responder Freestyle beyond the theology and boundaries your ministry has approved When Grace Is a Strong Fit If your team is losing time to repetitive inbound communication, visitor questions, spiritual first-touch conversations, or after-hours engagement, Grace is usually the right place to start. Downloadable Resources Grace Setup Guide (PDF guide) Grace Setup Guide Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Meet Grace - Your Spiritual Support Assistant

articles.body: Configuring Grace for Your Ministry Grace should sound like your ministry, not like a generic assistant. Configuration is less about tweaking lots of settings and more about giving Grace the right source material, the right boundaries, and the right escalation path. Start with These Inputs Your statement of faith or doctrinal guidance Current sermon transcripts, teaching notes, or ministry FAQs Guidance on tone, warmth, and language style Instructions for what Grace should hand off instead of answer directly Decisions to Make Before Launch Which questions Grace can answer directly Which questions should receive a short answer plus a handoff Which topics should always go to a pastor or team member Which channels Grace should support first Recommended Rollout Launch one channel first. Test common visitor and member scenarios. Test one or two sensitive scenarios to confirm handoff behavior. Review outputs with your leadership team before broad promotion. If Grace Feels Off If Grace sounds too generic, too cautious, or too confident, the fix is usually in the source material or boundary definitions rather than in cosmetic wording alone. Downloadable Resources Grace AI Ministry Training Guide (PDF guide) Grace AI Ministry Training Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Configuring Grace for Your Ministry

articles.body: Grace - Prayer Support and Biblical Guidance Grace can help your ministry respond warmly when someone needs prayer, encouragement, or a first point of biblical support. The healthiest use of this capability is guided, bounded, and anchored to your ministry's voice. Where Grace Can Add Value Providing a compassionate first response when someone asks for prayer Pointing people toward scripture-aligned encouragement rooted in your ministry context Helping your team avoid missed messages or delayed first-touch responses Where Caution Is Needed Complex pastoral care situations Mental health distress, trauma, or crisis language Doctrinal edge cases your ministry wants handled by staff Any moment where prayer support needs to become direct human follow-up Recommended Setup Define the tone you want for prayer responses. Load trusted ministry materials that reflect your theology and pastoral style. Set a clear handoff path for situations that move beyond simple encouragement. Review examples regularly with your team. Best Practice Grace is strongest when she offers a calm, ministry-aligned first response and then hands off cleanly when the conversation needs a person. Downloadable Resources Grace Triage, Prayer, and Crisis Escalation Guide (PDF guide) Grace Triage, Prayer, and Crisis Escalation Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Grace - Prayer Support and Biblical Guidance

articles.body: Connecting Grace to Your Website, Email, Text, and Chat Channels Grace is positioned as a digital front door for ministry communication. To be useful in day-to-day operations, she needs to be connected to the channels your congregation and visitors actually use. Typical Channel Rollout Website chat or website embed for guest questions and front-door engagement Email for administrative follow-up and common ministry inquiries Text / SMS for faster conversational support where your ministry uses it Other supported intake channels such as app or messaging workflows tied to your ministry setup Before You Connect a Channel Make sure your foundational docs and FAQs are current Decide what Grace should answer automatically versus what should go to staff Write or confirm your default handoff message for sensitive situations Identify who should be notified when a conversation needs a human follow-up Launch Checklist Connect one channel first and validate the behavior Test common admin questions, visitor questions, and one sensitive handoff case Confirm the assistant identifies itself clearly and responds in the right tone Confirm routed conversations reach the right team or workflow Add more channels only after the first one feels stable Common Signs a Channel Needs Attention Messages arrive slowly or not at all Grace answers in the wrong tone or with missing context Too many requests are being handed to staff Too few requests are being escalated Users are asking the same unanswered question repeatedly What to Include in a Channel Support Ticket Tell us which channel is affected, when the issue started, what the person sent, what Grace did, and what should have happened instead. Downloadable Resources Grace Website Chat Integration Guide (PDF guide) Grace Website Chat Integration Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Connecting Grace to Your Website, Email, Text, and Chat Channels

articles.body: Configuring Crisis Triage and Pastoral Escalation in Grace Grace should never be left to improvise on urgent pastoral or safety-sensitive situations. The healthiest setup is one where she can respond gently, recognize risk, and route the person to the right human path quickly. Conversations That Usually Need Escalation Self-harm or suicide language Abuse, immediate danger, or emergency situations Severe grief, trauma, or mental health distress Requests for counseling, discipline, or highly personal pastoral care Complex theological conflict your ministry wants handled by a pastor What to Configure Keywords and risk signals that should trigger a handoff Default handoff language that is compassionate, calm, and clear Emergency guidance including when to direct someone to emergency services or a crisis hotline Staff notification path so the right person is alerted after a flag After-hours expectations so your team knows what Grace should do outside normal availability Recommended Review Process Test a small set of crisis and non-crisis scenarios internally Confirm Grace escalates the right situations without sounding cold or robotic Review handoff language with your pastoral team Re-test after any major change to your documents or policies Important Boundary Grace can support triage and first-line care communication, but she is not a replacement for pastoral judgment, licensed counseling, or emergency response. Need Help Tuning This? Open a support ticket with the category Security / Compliance or AI Response / Content Issue and include the exact scenario you want reviewed.
articles.title: Configuring Crisis Triage and Pastoral Escalation in Grace

articles.body: Meet Levi - Your Ministry Operations Assistant Levi is SoulShine's internal operations and content assistant. He is built to help ministry teams move faster on planning, writing, and communications work that often consumes the week before anyone reaches the congregation-facing moment. Where Levi Usually Helps Most Sermon-aligned discipleship and content drafting Communication planning and campaign prep Research and structured brainstorming for ministry initiatives Reducing repetitive writing workload for staff What Makes Levi Better Clear examples of your ministry's tone and style Current sermon themes and doctrinal framing Specific prompts about audience, format, and desired outcome Human review before publishing important material What Levi Is Not For Publishing sensitive or high-stakes content without review Guessing your ministry voice from scratch Replacing leadership judgment on theology or pastoral messaging When Levi Is the Right Starting Product If your team is caught in a constant cycle of content production, campaign prep, newsletter writing, or sermon follow-up material, Levi is usually the best first fit. Downloadable Resources Levi Quick Start Guide (PDF guide) Levi Quick Start Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Meet Levi - Your Ministry Operations Assistant

articles.body: Using Levi for Content Creation Levi works best when your team treats him as a ministry content accelerator, not as a one-click publishing engine. The strongest results come when you provide clear source material, define the audience, and keep a human in the review loop. Content Types Levi Commonly Supports Sermon-based discussion guides Devotionals and discipleship material Email drafts, announcement copy, and follow-up sequences Social content and campaign messaging How to Get Better Output Start with the sermon title, passage, and core takeaway Name the audience clearly, such as visitors, small-group leaders, parents, or volunteers Tell Levi the format you want, not just the topic Provide a few examples of what good ministry content already sounds like in your context Recommended Review Flow Generate the first draft. Check theology, tone, and accuracy. Tighten language for your specific ministry culture. Only publish after a human review step. Support Tip If Levi's drafts feel shallow or off-tone, include the exact prompt and output in your support ticket. That makes it much easier to diagnose whether the issue is source material, prompt quality, or expectations.
articles.title: Using Levi for Content Creation

articles.body: Levi - Outreach Planning and Analytics Levi can help your team think more strategically about ministry communications and outreach planning. The goal is not just to make more content. The goal is to clarify who you are trying to reach, what message fits that audience, and which follow-up steps matter most. Where Levi Is Useful in Outreach Planning Structuring campaign themes and messaging Turning one ministry initiative into multiple communications assets Helping teams prepare for event promotion, volunteer invites, or guest follow-up Summarizing what seems to be working or not working across repeated communication patterns Questions to Answer Before Using Levi Strategically Who is the audience? What response do we want from them? What should be clarified, emphasized, or avoided? Who will own the follow-through after the message is sent? Best Practice Use Levi to sharpen your planning and messaging, then let ministry leaders apply context, discernment, and final judgment. Strategy is strongest when AI helps organize the work without pretending to own it.
articles.title: Levi - Outreach Planning and Analytics

articles.body: Managing Your SoulShine AI Account Account management should stay simple and disciplined. The main goals are to keep ownership clear, limit access to the right people, and make sure the ministry's core details stay current. Account Areas to Review Regularly Primary account email and billing ownership Ministry profile and public-facing organization details Who has access to assistant configuration or sensitive workflows Notification and follow-up expectations for support or escalations Good Operational Habits Use a real long-term ministry email for ownership whenever possible Remove access promptly when roles change Keep ministry context updated after leadership or schedule changes Document who approves billing and who approves assistant behavior changes When to Contact Support Open an Account Access or Billing Question ticket if you need help with account ownership, login issues, permissions confusion, or subscription-linked account changes.
articles.title: Managing Your SoulShine AI Account

articles.body: Updating Your Profile Keeping your profile information current helps us serve you better. Here is how to manage your SoulShine AI profile settings. Changing Your Display Name Log in to your SoulShine AI account at soulshineai.com Click your profile icon in the top-right corner Select Account Settings Update your Display Name field Click Save Changes Updating Your Email Address Navigate to Account Settings > Profile Click Change Email Enter your new email address You will receive a verification email at the new address click the confirmation link to complete the change Managing Notification Preferences Control which notifications you receive: Email Notifications: Weekly ministry insights, product updates, and prayer community activity In-App Notifications: Real-time alerts when Grace or Levi complete tasks Digest Frequency: Choose daily or weekly summary emails To adjust these settings, go to Account Settings > Notifications and toggle each option on or off. Setting Your Time Zone Your time zone affects when you receive scheduled content and how timestamps appear. Set it under Account Settings > Preferences > Time Zone.
articles.title: How to Update Your Profile and Notification Preferences

articles.body: Account Deletion and Data Export We respect your right to manage your personal data. This guide covers how to export your data and, if needed, delete your SoulShine AI account. Exporting Your Data Before deleting your account, you may want to save a copy of your content: Go to Account Settings > Privacy & Data Click Request Data Export You will receive a download link via email within 24 hours The export includes your conversation history, saved content, and profile information in a portable format Deleting Your Account Important: Account deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. All your data, including conversation history with Grace and Levi, saved ministry content, and preferences will be permanently removed. Go to Account Settings > Privacy & Data Click Delete My Account Enter your password to confirm Review the summary of what will be deleted Click Permanently Delete Account What Happens After Deletion Your account is immediately deactivated All personal data is purged within 30 days Any active subscription is canceled and prorated You will receive a final confirmation email Need Help? If you are having issues with your account and considering deletion, please reach out to our support team first. We may be able to help resolve your concerns. Contact us at support@soulshineai.com.
articles.title: How to Delete Your Account and Export Your Data

articles.body: Subscription Plans and Billing SoulShine offers multiple product paths for ministries, including Grace, Levi, Thriving Groups, and the broader Soul 247 offering. The most accurate pricing and trial details should always be confirmed on the live Plans page. Before You Choose a Plan Decide whether your immediate need is front-office support, content creation, discipleship workflows, or a broader digital ministry team Start with the product that solves your most urgent operational bottleneck first Confirm current pricing, trial length, and package details on the SoulShine plans page Billing and Subscription Questions Use support if you need help understanding which offering fits your ministry Use support if you need account-level help tied to billing, access, or subscription changes Keep a record of the ministry account email used during signup when contacting support Where to Get Help For billing or subscription questions, open a support ticket and choose Billing Question. Replies come from support@soulshineai.com. Downloadable Resources Billing, Trial, and Cancellation FAQ (PDF guide) Billing, Trial, and Cancellation Video Tutorial (video tutorial download) These files open or download directly from the support portal. Watch The Video Tutorial
articles.title: Subscription Plans and Billing

articles.body: Non-Profit and Church Discount Programs SoulShine is built for ministry organizations, so discount and plan-fit questions usually come down to ministry size, current workload, and the product path that will create the most immediate value. Public plan and pricing details should always be confirmed against the live Plans page. Before Asking About Discount Eligibility Know which product path you are considering first Be clear about ministry size and the team problem you are trying to solve Be ready to explain whether the main need is congregation support, content, discipleship, or a broader digital ministry workflow How to Handle Pricing Questions Use the live Plans page for current public pricing. Use a Billing Question ticket if you need help interpreting which option fits your ministry. Include your organization type and the product you are evaluating so the reply can be specific. Important Note If your ministry needs pricing clarification, quote review, or account-specific billing help, contact support@soulshineai.com through the support system so the conversation stays tied to your account history.
articles.title: Non-Profit and Church Discount Programs

articles.body: Choosing the Right SoulShine Product for Your Ministry SoulShine's public site describes several product paths. The best fit depends on whether your immediate pressure is front-office communication, back-office content creation, group discipleship, or a broader ministry operations stack. When Grace Is the Best Starting Point You need a 24/7 digital front door Your team is overloaded with routine questions, visitor replies, or care intake You want faster response times without adding staff headcount When Levi Is the Best Starting Point You need sermon-aligned content, curriculum drafts, newsletters, or social assets Your team is stuck in the weekly content treadmill You want more strategic planning and research support behind the scenes When Thriving Groups Makes Sense You want sermon-to-discussion workflows for leaders and small groups You need group guides, leader help, and mid-week discipleship continuity You want better volunteer confidence without adding prep burden When Soul 247 Team Is the Better Fit You want a broader digital ministry team rather than a single assistant You need both front-office engagement and back-office content support You want a more unified operational setup across communication, care, and content How to Decide Identify the one problem costing your team the most time right now Choose the product that solves that pain first Roll out one workflow successfully before adding more complexity Use the public Plans page for the latest pricing and trial details If You Are Unsure Open a General Question or Billing Question ticket and tell us your ministry size, your biggest operational bottleneck, and whether you need more help with community care, content, discipleship, or the full digital front door.
articles.title: Choosing the Right SoulShine Product for Your Ministry

articles.body: Grace Not Responding or Slow Responses If Grace is slow, inconsistent, or not replying as expected, start by narrowing the problem. Most issues fall into one of three buckets: the channel is not delivering messages correctly, Grace does not have enough context to respond well, or a routing or availability rule is preventing the expected behavior. What to Check First Which channel is affected: website, email, text, or another connected intake path Whether the problem affects every conversation or only certain message types Whether Grace is failing to reply at all or replying with poor quality or long delays Common Causes Channel connection issues or delayed message delivery Incomplete foundational documents or weak ministry context Guardrails that are causing Grace to defer too often After-hours or handoff expectations that are not configured clearly What to Include in a Support Ticket The affected channel The approximate time the issue occurred The exact message or prompt that was sent What Grace did What should have happened instead Best Practice Send one concrete example instead of a general complaint. A real example is the fastest way to separate a delivery issue from a tone, safety, or grounding issue.
articles.title: Grace Not Responding or Slow Responses

articles.body: Content Generation Issues with Levi If Levi's content is weak, repetitive, off-tone, or unusable, the problem is usually not that the system needs more volume. The problem is usually unclear source material, vague prompting, or an audience and format that have not been defined clearly enough. Common Problems The output is too generic The tone does not sound like your ministry The content misses the sermon emphasis or key takeaway The structure is wrong for the audience or channel What Usually Improves Results Provide the sermon title, passage, and one-sentence central takeaway Name the target audience clearly Specify the exact format you want Provide examples of past ministry content that reflect the right tone When to Contact Support Open an AI Response / Content Issue ticket if Levi repeatedly produces poor results even after you clarify the prompt and source material. Include the prompt, the output, and what needed to be different.
articles.title: Content Generation Issues with Levi

articles.body: Login and Access Issues If you cannot access SoulShine, focus on ownership, credentials, and browser state first. Most access problems are straightforward once you identify whether the issue is tied to the account email, the individual user, or an expired session. Quick Checks Confirm you are using the correct account email Try the standard password reset flow if applicable Check spam or junk folders for reset emails Try a private browser window or a second browser to rule out stale session issues When to Open Support You no longer have access to the original account owner inbox A reset email never arrives You believe access or ownership needs to be transferred A staff change created permission confusion What to Include in the Ticket Provide the ministry name, the affected login email, whether this is an ownership issue or a normal login issue, and any relevant timing or error details. Use the Account Access ticket type.
articles.title: Login and Access Issues

articles.body: Understanding Error Messages Error messages are most useful when you treat them as context, not as the full diagnosis. In SoulShine, most errors will point to one of a few broad issue types: authentication, connectivity, missing context, or blocked behavior tied to safety and workflow rules. Useful Questions to Ask What were you trying to do when the error appeared? Did this happen once or repeatedly? Is the issue tied to one channel, one user, or one workflow? Did anything change right before the issue started? What to Capture Before Contacting Support The exact error message text A screenshot if one is available The channel or workflow involved The time the issue occurred The result you expected instead Best Practice Do not paraphrase the error if you can avoid it. The exact message and the workflow around it are usually enough for support to narrow the issue quickly.
articles.title: Understanding Error Messages

articles.body: Resolving Connection and Loading Issues If you encounter connection problems or pages that fail to load, follow these troubleshooting steps. "Unable to Connect" or "Network Error" These errors typically indicate a connectivity issue between your device and SoulShine AI servers. Steps to resolve: Check your internet connection try loading another website to confirm Refresh the page or restart the app Clear your browser cache: Settings > Privacy > Clear Browsing Data Try a different browser or device Disable any VPN or proxy that may be interfering "Page Not Loading" or Blank Screen Browser extensions Ad blockers or privacy extensions can sometimes block required resources. Try disabling them for soulshineai.com Outdated browser SoulShine AI works best on the latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge JavaScript disabled Ensure JavaScript is enabled in your browser settings "Request Timed Out" This means the server took too long to respond. Common causes: High traffic periods try again in a few minutes Long or complex requests to Grace or Levi try breaking your request into smaller parts Slow internet connection switch to a stronger WiFi network or cellular data "Service Temporarily Unavailable" (503 Error) This indicates scheduled maintenance or unexpected high demand. Check our status page or social media channels for updates. Service is typically restored within minutes. Still Having Issues? If problems persist after trying these steps, contact our support team with: The exact error message you see Your browser name and version The device and operating system you are using The time the error occurred
articles.title: Connection and Loading Errors

articles.body: When Grace or Levi Are Not Responding as Expected Occasionally, our AI assistants may produce unexpected responses or encounter errors. Here is how to troubleshoot common AI-related issues. "I did not understand that" or No Response If Grace or Levi cannot process your request: Rephrase your question Try asking in a different way or breaking complex questions into simpler parts Check for special characters Avoid using excessive symbols or formatting that may confuse the AI Start a new conversation If a conversation becomes stuck, starting fresh often resolves the issue Response Seems Off-Topic or Inaccurate AI assistants may occasionally misinterpret context: Provide more context Include relevant background information in your request Specify which assistant you need Grace handles spiritual guidance and devotional content; Levi handles ministry operations and administrative tasks Use the feedback button Rate responses to help improve future accuracy "Content Generation Failed" This can occur when requesting very long or complex content: Try requesting shorter content first, then build upon it Be more specific about what you need (e.g., "Write a 200-word devotional about patience" instead of "Write me something") Wait a moment and try again the system may have been temporarily at capacity Response Cut Off or Incomplete If a response ends abruptly: Ask the assistant to "continue" or "finish the previous response" Request the content in sections (e.g., "Give me the first 3 points, then I will ask for the rest") Safety Filter Triggered SoulShine AI includes guardrails to ensure content aligns with our faith-based values. If your request was flagged: Review our Safety and Guardrails guide in the Knowledge Base Rephrase your request to be more specific about the ministry context Contact support if you believe the filter was triggered in error
articles.title: AI Response Errors and Unexpected Behavior

articles.body: Creating Sermon-Aligned Content with AI Sermon alignment is one of the clearest ministry use cases for SoulShine. The goal is not to mass-produce generic copy. The goal is to carry one message faithfully across multiple touchpoints without forcing your team to rewrite everything from scratch each week. Good Inputs for Sermon-Aligned Work Sermon title and scripture passage The main takeaway or central burden of the message Any phrases, themes, or theological emphases you want preserved The audience and format needed next Common Outputs Small-group discussion guides Devotionals and follow-up reflection prompts Email and announcement drafts Social posts and campaign support copy Best Workflow Start with the sermon source material. Generate only the formats you actually need. Review for theology, tone, and clarity. Adjust for your ministry's audience before publishing. What to Avoid Do not ask Levi to approximate a sermon theme from memory when you already have the real notes or transcript. Direct source material is almost always better.
articles.title: Creating Sermon-Aligned Content with AI

articles.body: Social Media Strategy for Ministries Social media is most effective for ministry when it supports a real discipleship or communication goal instead of becoming a volume game. Levi can help your team turn one message into multiple formats, but the strategy still needs to be intentional. Start with Purpose, Not Platform Are you trying to invite, encourage, teach, or follow up? Who is the audience for this post or sequence? What should they do next after engaging with it? Where Levi Can Help Turning sermon themes into short-form content Building a weekly or event-based content plan Drafting multiple versions of the same message for different channels Keeping language consistent across posts, emails, and announcements Good Ministry Practice Keep the tone aligned with your church or ministry voice Do not post sensitive pastoral material without review Let social content support real ministry next steps, not replace them
articles.title: Social Media Strategy for Ministries

articles.body: Growing Your Congregation with AI Insights Healthy ministry growth is not just about scale. It is about clarity, responsiveness, and following up well with the people God has already placed in front of your church or organization. SoulShine can help surface patterns and reduce response friction, but leaders still need to interpret the signals wisely. What AI Insights Are Best Used For Spotting repeated questions or communication bottlenecks Seeing where follow-up is breaking down Understanding which messages or resources are resonating Freeing staff time so relational ministry gets more attention What Not to Do Do not treat dashboards as a substitute for pastoral discernment. Do not optimize only for volume if clarity and care are declining. Do not assume every communication metric represents spiritual health. Best Practice Use insights to identify friction points, then respond with better follow-up, clearer messaging, and stronger ministry processes.
articles.title: Growing Your Congregation with AI Insights

articles.body: Setting Up 24/7 Spiritual Support One of Grace's strongest roles is helping ministries stay present after staff hours without pretending that AI can replace real pastoral care. A healthy 24/7 setup is one where people receive a warm first response, know what Grace can and cannot do, and are clearly routed to human help when needed. What a Healthy Always-On Setup Includes A clear introduction so people understand they are talking with Grace Trusted ministry context and foundational documents Defined boundaries for crisis, abuse, self-harm, and urgent care needs A real human handoff process for the situations Grace should not handle alone Before You Promote 24/7 Support Test common questions and prayer scenarios. Test at least one crisis-style escalation case internally. Confirm your after-hours messaging is accurate. Make sure your team knows what follow-up Grace may generate. Important Boundary Always-on support should increase care access, not create the illusion that no human follow-up is ever needed. Grace should support the first response and triage layer, while your ministry owns the deeper care response.
articles.title: Setting Up 24/7 Spiritual Support
